requirements:
Required Courses

2.5Units to Complete
  • Complete all of the following
    • Complete all the following:
      • AVIA100 - Introduction to Aviation (0.50)
    • Complete 4 of the following:
      • AVIA210 - Human Factors in Aviation (0.50)
      • AVIA270 - Remotely Piloted Aircraft Systems (RPAS) Knowledge Requirements (0.50)
      • AVIA317 - Aviation Safety (0.50)
      • AVIA370 - Aviation Meteorology (0.50)
      • AVIA374 - Special Topics in Aviation (0.50)
      • AVIA416 - Aviation Sustainability (0.50)
      • AVIA470 - Airline Planning and Management (0.50)
      • AVIA474 - Special Topics in Aviation (0.50)
      • AVIA475 - Independent Studies of Selected Topics (0.50)
      • GDS270 - Remotely Piloted Aircraft Systems (RPAS) Knowledge Requirements (0.50)
      • GEOG351 - Transportation, Mobility, and Sustainability (0.50)

Grand Total Units: 2.5
additionalConstraints:
1. Students may only complete one course from any cross-listed set.
  2. Students who have either successfully completed or received transfer credit for AVIA121 (0.50 unit), and/or AVIA141 (0.25 unit) and AVIA142 (0.25 unit), may use these courses towards the four of course requirement. Note that credit must have been received for both AVIA141 (0.25 unit) and AVIA142 (0.25 unit) to count as one course towards this requirement. Proof of a valid Transport Canada Pilot Licence must be provided to the Aviation Program Manager for transfer credit assessment.
title: Aviation Specialization
declarationRequirements: • It is recommended that students declare the addition of a specialization as early as possible by submitting a Plan Modification Form.
